The Angkor Conservation Office (then run by the French) opted to act. Although invasive, such intervention was an appropriate overall response to the needs of the monument. The technical options selected were a reflection of the experience that the The Ecole Francaise d’Extreme Orient had gathered from its work on the monument dating back to the early years of the century. The “anastylosis” technique—meaning the reconstruction of a ruin using its own components—was the bottom line.
